Change entry from compact to classic

Select visible

Hope you find your prefered settings. Somehow this gets overwritten from time to time. This is fast way to change things.

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Hello @ivelinakiryakov ,

There can be several role collections that have the same displayName in multiple BTP subaccounts. When provisioning these from the BTP Subaccount to the Local Identity Directory I want these role collections seperate entitites , so that they can be managed separately.  Before the concept of the applicationId, I already used the xsuaa.group.prefix property in the source system to make them distinguishable and unique. 

Now with the concept of the applicationId, I was under the impression that you could use the applicationId as an attribute of "uniqueness"  that determines if the group / role collection gets created instead of updated.  The current attribute supported for idds.group.unique.attribute is only displayName... 

This results in:

In your previous answer you mentioned that you would still need to use the xsuaa.group.prefix property in the source system to get this behaviour.

To me it seems counter-intuitive that the applicationId is not used for (co-)determining the uniqueness in the local identity directory. 

The same with after making group assignments and performing provisioning of these users and group assignments to several BTP subaccounts from the Local Identity Directory as source. I used the  xsuaa.group.prefix property in the target systems to provision only role collections with the prefix in the displayname. 

Thsi results in the skipped entries... Would it not be nicer if the Groups / Role Collections from subaccounts with a different ApplicationId are not provisioned at all, instead of skipped on condition?

If I am overlooking the obvious, my appologies....

entry.title: QTD functionality on Group Financial Statements Review Booklet app
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to create a report in group reporting, in the review booklet app. More specifically the Group Financial Statements in Group Reporting Public Cloud edition.

I am having issues when displaying the data by quarter to date.

The report offers various fields in the Available Objects - Structure section and I was able to find the field "Closing Quarter P/YYYY". However, this field retrieves the data for the entire quarter instead of from the first period of the quarter to the period selected in the report filters.

Example, if my Quarter 1 goes from Jan to March and I want my report to retrieve February data, my expectation of the QTD is to retrieve January + February data. However, the field "Closing Quarter P/YYYY" in the Group Financial Statements Review Booklet app pulls January + February + March. 

How can I obtain the field QTD-Quarter to date where the field retrieves the aggregated value of the first period of the quarter to the actual reporting period?

I have also tried creating the field using the "Add Calculation" functions, but I couldn't make it work due to the lack of necessary fields.

Is there another report in Group Reporting where the field QTD-Quarter to date (or Closing quarter to date) could be available?

Thank you.

entry.title: The AI Adoption Journey: Machine Learning
entry.content:
Hello and Happy 2025! Thank you all for joining me on the AI Adoption Journey!


Last we spoke, we went over some basics about AI, it's main purpose and how it's far more accessible and integrated in many services and applications we use today! AI usage shaves of seconds and minutes of time spent processing, and while convenience is an obvious benefit, the time saved adds up significantly.

We left off after unwrapping the mystery around Machine Learning, and I'm excited to talk about how SAP uses Machine Learning specifically to help save time and boost the quality of workforce management.

So, how does SAP use machine learning? Back in 2023, SAP Fieldglass automatically enabled two features called Resume Ranking, Skills Extraction and for new buyers, and the option to have it enabled by a Configuration Manager for existing buyers.

 

Let's talk about what the AI for both of these features really do. Resume ranking combs through the resume submitted for a candidate and matches it against the details on the job description, and rank the submissions numerically, on how closely they match the job posting they were submitted to.

This seems straightforward enough, but let me pitch a few scenarios at you:

You are managing an office location, and your server manager is on emergency medical leave. You need to fill their position as soon as possible! You log into Fieldglass, create a job posting, and send it to a substantial list of suppliers that you are sure will yield a decent substitute.


As the submissions start coming in and adding up, you realize that you now have 20-30 candidates to sift through, in addition to managing all of your responsibilities as well as the server manager's. A lot of time is lost on pouring over resumes to find the best fit!

AI steps in with the resume ranking to get the most important pieces of information in front of you. 

Let's say that you have 10 or so candidates ranked number 1 and have some difficulty discerning between them. The Skills Extraction feature has the ability to analyze the resume, extract skills and relevant experience and display them as percentages on the modal.

 

A seemingly simple feature like this can shave off hours of time in the hiring process without compromising quality. 

With these features, the buyer can also get recommendations from their existing workforce and workers that are similar to a referenced record. The tool will recommend candidates by comparing the resumes to the referenced record, using the candidate's work history, skill set and location.

Something interesting I also found is any candidates who registered in the company's candidate portal that have resumes available on their profiles will also see selectable tile on their home page called "Job Match". This will allow them to apply to a list of jobs that match the information on their resume with the job description.
 

These are all features under Machine Learning, and they all come with Fieldglass's base AI features that are available to everyone. They are self-service, meaning they can be enabled by the Configuration Manager (no long SLAs are ticket times!) and help companies boost their workforce significantly by saving time without the cost of quality workers, and having an automated process that promotes fair and data driven hiring.


The same technology that recommends the best music, movies, books and media to our tastes can do the same for our companies by recommending the best candidates in available to us.

For more information, please check out the SAP Fieldglass Product Work Zone. They have an amazing AI Hub where you can learn more and interact with the features through different personas: SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone - AI Hub


Also, please check out this great series by Fabian Caelers on Quick Win Features to learn some more valuable tips and tricks to make the most of your solution: SAP Fieldglass - Quick Win Features - Episode 2 - SAP Community


Join me next time for a deeper dive into generative AI!

entry.title: Credit Management for Advanced Execution Order Item in Financial and Contract Accounting (FICA)
entry.content:
This blog will help you to understand how business process works when it comes to the financial and contract accounting scenario in advanced execution order item.

In the billing solution adapted for S/4 Hana Service Transactions, known as Service Orders (SO) will be via Dynamic Item Processor Framework (DIP) [transaction DP92], During this process a Debit Memo Request(DMR) document is generated. The DMR that is generated out of DIP could qualify for Credit Commitment update, Hence Credit management integration process needs to be triggered, Following are the key categories where credit exposures movement takes place.Transaction code to view Credit Exposures: “UKM_COMMITMENTS”. 

Type of Credit Exposures Category

Description of Credit Exposures Category

110

Service Order & Service Contract

410

Service Confirmations

500

Billing Document Value

200

Open Invoices

There are different billing relevance in service order for which credit management gets triggered.

In this blog we will be focusing  on how credit management process takes place in FI-CA enabled system. Behaviour is the same for summarized and itemized billing relevance. In this blog will be learning the end to end  process via summarized billing relevance.

Customizing for credit check:

SPRO---Service--- Transactions---- Basic Settings----- Credit Management----

Make sure the transaction type is credit check enabled.

Make sure the service order item category is also credit check enabled.

Check the DIP Profile Setting: Transaction “ODP1” .

In FICA enabled system, Steps to activate the FICA Integration:

Summarized Billing Relevance:

To create the service order user must assigned the service manager role to their user: SAP_BR_SERVICE_MANAGER.

Once the role is assigned to the user profile. Manage Service Order’s app can be accessible.

Create the service order with advanced execution order item and add the billing relevance as “Summarized Billing” and enter the DIP Profile.

After creating the service order, user must manually release the item. On released action credit check process is getting triggered to check whether the user is having sufficient credit to create the new service order or to release the item.Further integration with the Credit Management process gets trigger and update the credit exposure commitments in the 110 (Service Order & Service Contract) categories.  

Credit commitment exposure in 110 category on service order released.

Plant Maintenance Order (PMO) will be created upon successful release of the advanced execution order item. Manual release of the PMO in transaction via “IW32”.

Posting PMO Confirmations in transaction via IW48 to create the Debit Memo Request. Adding the actual work details in IW48 and saving the entry. Go to DP92 and create the debit memo request (DMR).

Debit Memo Request (DMR) is Created.

Credit commitment exposures should get updated in 410 categories on DMR creation.

Create the Debit Memo.

As user created the debit memo, hence credit commitment exposures will move from 410 to 500 category.

Check the 410 Commitment. As the 410 exposures moved to 500 category on successful creation of debit memo. No value should persist in the 410 category

While creating the debit memo (Invoice),Invoice values will automatically send to accounting. Manual release to accounting not needed but to transfer the credit commitment data to 200 category (Open Invoices) user should follow the below steps:

Credit Management is not updated each time an open item in FI-CA is posted.Instead, FI-CA collects all the data relevant for Credit Management (on business partner level) and hands this information over to Credit Management using mass activity “Transfer Credit Data” (TA FPCM1).

Once user create the invoice (VF01). Entry will get updated to the “DFKKOBLID - Partner Messages for SAP Credit Management Update” Table.

Before user run the transaction to transfer the data to 200 category, Note the earlier commitment: Commitment Value : 246.910,97

Run the transaction “FPCM1”.

Enter the Date ID : The date on which user want to schedule the job to transfer the commitment data.

Run ID                 : Enter the uniquely identifies program run id .

Business Partner : Enter the Business Partner and select the below checkboxes as shown in below image.

Go to logs and the Problem Class : Additional Information

Save the Program Run and Execute the Schedule Program Run.

User will get the Job Scheduling Option: Select one of the option to set the Job.

Once the Program run is Completed. Check the table DFKKOBLID. No entries will exist in the table as Credit data is already moved to 200 category.No entry persists in the table.

Check the Credit Commitment Data in the 200 Category. New records stored in the table DFKKOBLID table moved to 200 category on successful program run.

Check the credit commitment date in 500 category. As all the credit data has been transferred to 200 category, all the credit commitment data removed from 500 category.

Steps to follow when Invoice is cancelled.

Cancel the invoice 90014452.

Cancel Invoice (S1) entry will get updated under transaction history tab.During cancellation of the FI-CA document, FI-CA also stores a “partner message” in table DFKKOBLID.

The ID of the partner message is the ID of the SD billing document.

If TA FPCM1 is executed with the parameters marked in below image , these entries from table DFKKOBLID are considered. This information helps FI-CA to know, which commitments need to be reduced.

Once the Program Run is Completed. Check the credit commitment date in 200 category.  65 USD has been reduced from 246,910.97 USD

This is how  credit management works in advanced execution order item in service order for FICA enabled system.

entry.title: Re: Bring Open-Source LLMs into SAP AI Core
entry.content:
Hi @Srina, Thanks for your feedback.

Here you have my answer to your inquiry about BYOM self-hosted LLM from private data center into SAP AI Core, such as a private LLM or fine-tuning models.

In this case, llama.cpp and  vLLM are recommended to serve these self-hosted LLMs in private data center. Logically speaking, two steps are needed when starting a new deployment of llama.cpp and vLLM in SAP AI Core.
Step 1: Download the self-hosted LLMs from private data center with wget tool into the local folder in the SAP AI Core runtime container.
Step 2: Serve the self-hosted LLM downloaded in step 1 with llama.cpp and vLLM through an absolute local model path.

Option 1: llama.cpp

If the self-hosted LLMs in private data center could be downloaded through wget tool, then you can download the llms, then start the llama.cpp server to serve when create a deplopyment of the serving template for llama.cpp in SAP AI Core.

Here you have the sample code in serving template of SAP AI Core for llama.cpp: https://github.com/SAP-samples/btp-generative-ai-hub-use-cases/blob/main/10-byom-oss-llm-ai-core/byom-oss-llm-templates/llama.cpp-template.yaml#L79

If you need to specify the user name and password to download the self-hosted llm from your private data center, then you can use the command: wget --user xxx ---password … Please refer to the manual of wget command for details.

 In addition, you may introduce configurable input parameters for user and password in the serving template.

Option 2: vLLM

The same approach can be applicable to vLLM too with wget tool to download the  self-hosted llm from private data center.

Just to update vllm-template.yaml as below:

1). Adding an input parameter as modelDownloadUrl. Simply copy it from here
2). Adapt the starting script in vllm-template.yaml as

&& wget -P /hf-home -tries=10 {{inputs.parameters.modelDownloadUrl}}  
&&  python3 -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model  /hf-home/the-absoluate-model-file-path # instead of the model id in HuggingFace as  {{inputs.parameters.modelName}}, use the absoluate model path downloaded with wget command above.

Kind Regards,
Yatsea

entry.title: Re: Set parameter id storage capacity
entry.content:
Hello @Praveen_Sankaraddi 

As per SAP Help SET PARAMETER ID pid FIELD dobj can throw exceptions i.a.:

Cause: Key is longer than 20 characters
Runtime Error: SET_PARAMETER_ID_TOO_LONG

Cause: Value is longer than 255 characters
Runtime Error: SET_PARAMETER_VALUE_TOO_LONG

That means that the PID is limited to 20 chars and the value (DOBJ) is limited to 255 chars.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Building Dynamic Pop Ups using oDataV4 RAP Service in Fiori applications
entry.content:
INTRODUCTION

In modern Web Applications Dynamic Pop Up is a very common scenario.As a user we want to provide some details for an action to be executed on document. It could be a Comment or some reference number etc.

In this blog we are showing a scenario where depending on

Purchase Order Type the "Release " action requires different parameters.e.g. For a Standard PO (NB), only a comment is required, however for a Stock Transfer (STO) user needs to also provide a Reference number.

This is a simple requirement, however the solution we are going to cover in this blog is applicable for complex real world scenario as well.

Giving you a sneak peek into what we are going to achieve.

Prerequisite

1.Set up a Dev Space in Business Application Studio.

2.You have setup a user in SAP BTP ABAP environment.

3.You have downloaded and installed latest ABAP Development tools(ADT)

4. Basic understanding of SAPUI5 and RAP based Service.

Development Steps

STEP 1: Create Package ZPUR_ID# in you eclipse ADT environment.

STEP 2: Create Database Table ZPO_TBL

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Table for purchase order'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
@AbapCatalog.tableCategory : #TRANSPARENT
@AbapCatalog.deliveryClass : #A
@AbapCatalog.dataMaintenance : #RESTRICTED
define table zpo_tbl {

  key client            : abap.clnt not null;
  key po_num            : ebeln not null;
  org                   : ekorg;
  vendor                : lifnr;
  status                : abap.char(1);
  type                  : abap.char(2);
  reason                : abap.char(40);
  refno                 : abap.char(10);
  create_by             : abp_creation_user;
  created_date_time     : abp_creation_tstmpl;
  changed_date_time     : abp_locinst_lastchange_tstmpl;
  local_last_changed_by : abp_locinst_lastchange_user;

}

 

 

 

 

Step 3: Generate CDS Services ( Others objects ) using the wizard

This is a very useful feature. Press Next .

Click on Next and Finish.

Step 4: At this stage you can publish your service. 

Step 5 : Add a virtual Field

After Generation a new Virtual Field was added in CDS ZC_PO_TBL 

This is a boolean field and we are going to use it to manage visibility of parameters on action dialog.The logic for value of this field will be written in class ZCL_PO_CALC

Add below additional code to generated code for ZC_PO_TBL

 

 

  //Add New Virtual Element
  @ObjectModel.virtualElementCalculatedBy: 'ABAP:ZCL_PO_CALC'
  @EndUserText.label: 'Virtual Element'
  virtual HideAdditionalParams: abap_boolean,

 

 

Step 6: Create ABAP Class ZCL_PO_CALC

 

 

 

CLASS zcl_po_calc DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit .
    INTERFACES if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS zcl_po_calc IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~calculate.

    DATA: lt_calculated_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF zc_po_tbl.

    CHECK NOT it_original_data IS INITIAL.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING it_original_data TO lt_calculated_data.

    LOOP AT lt_calculated_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_data>).
      CASE <fs_data>-Type.
        WHEN 'NB'.
          <fs_data>-HideAdditionalParams = 'X'.
        WHEN 'ST'.
          <fs_data>-HideAdditionalParams = ''.
      ENDCASE.

    ENDLOOP.

    MOVE-CORRESPONDING lt_calculated_data TO ct_calculated_data.

  ENDMETHOD.


  METHOD if_sadl_exit_calc_element_read~get_calculation_info.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

The logic applied here is that when type of Purchase Order is NB then the action should allow only Remarks parameter. In case it is of type ST then show additional parameters .Please note this is a basic logic which can be replaced by actual business requirement.

Step 7: Create an abstract entity ZA_PO_PARAMS

 

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Abstract CDS for Demo Actions'
define abstract entity ZA_PO_PARAMS
 // with parameters parameter_name : parameter_type
{
    
    .hidden:true
    .defaultValue: #( 'ELEMENT_OF_REFERENCED_ENTITY: HideAdditionalParams' )
    HideAdditionalParameters: abap_boolean;
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Reference Number'
    .hidden: #(HideAdditionalParameters)
    ReferenceNumber: abap.char(10);
    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Remarks'
    .multiLineText : true
    Reason: abap.char(100);
    
}

 

 

Here we have added a field HideAdditionalParameters of type Boolean and getting it's value populated by the virtual field created earlier.ELEMENT_OF_REFERENCED_ENTITY is the keyword which allows access to values from the consumption CDS.This is a technical field and hence it is always hidden on UI.Next we have used this property to determine visibility of other property.

Step 8: Add Action in root entity Behaviour Definition ZR_PO_TBL

 

action Release parameter ZA_PO_PARAMS result [1] $self;

 

Step 9 : Refer to the action in Consumption Behaviour definition ZC_PO_TBL

 

use action Release;

 

 

Step 10 : Add UI annotations for actions. Go to Metadata Extension and open ZC_PO_TBL

 

.lineItem: [{ type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'Release', label: 'Release', importance: #HIGH, invocationGrouping: #CHANGE_SET }]

 

Following Objects were created.

Development Object TypeName
PackageZPUR_ID#
TableZPO_TBL
Root Entity CDSZR_PO_TBL
Consumption CDSZC_PO_TBL
Metadata ExtensionZC_PO_TBL
Behaviour Definition

ZC_PO_TBL

ZR_PO_TBL

Service DefinitionZUI_PO_TBL_O4
Service BindingZUI_PO_TBL_O4

Step 11 : These are our final steps to see the Fiori Application. Login to Business Application Studio.

Generate a Fiori application using Fiori Application Generation Wizard and selecting List Report from the available set of template. Select the oData Service we generated in previous steps as the data source.

Right click on your generated project in BAS and open page map.Click on add controller extension to your list report.We are adding controller extension as "HideAdditionalParams" being a technical field is not shown on the UI.At the same time we need to fetch it from backend for our logic to work.

Add highlighted code in generated extension controller file.

And we are done !!! You can preview the Fiori application.

Additional Tips: In case you face any issues , most common issue might be mismatch in some name. So review your code once. Sometimes just unpublishing and publishing the oData Service is helpful.

entry.title: SAP PAPM Cloud SM integration with SAP cTMS
entry.content:
At the moment, we can export and download as an environment content zip file using Content Management from SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud Standard Model (SAP PaPM Cloud SM) application, then upload and import the content into the subsequent tenant.

Although this feature remains functional, we observe that certain content managers are seeking an extra capability to control the movement of environments between, for example, DEV and PROD, who will adhere to audit norms. We heard you!

Therefore, in this blogpost allow me to introduce you to the much-awaited integration of SAP PaPM Cloud SM and SAP Cloud Transport Management (SAP cTMS)

What is SAP cTMS?

SAP Cloud Transport Management service (cTMS) lets you manage software deliverables between accounts of different environments (such as Cloud Foundry, ABAP, and Neo), by transporting them across various runtimes. This includes application artifacts as well as their respective application-specific content and adds transparency to the audit trail of changes so that you get information about who performed which changes in your production environment, and when they did it.

To understand more about cTMS, you can visit What Is SAP Cloud Transport Management?

Before we proceed further, as an admin/content manager you need to take note of these important points:

  1. SAP cTMS will not replace the existing Content Management; however, it will serve as an additional flavor for content managers when managing the content between tenants/
  2. SAP cTMS application is NOT included or bundled with SAP PaPM Cloud SM, therefore it is important to consider this information before deciding to use cTMS.
  3. A single cTMS subscription can be shared across multiple sub-accounts. For instance, a single Cloud Transport Management instance can be assigned as a new Destination in SAP BTP Cockpit for two or more different PaPM subaccounts from which you wish to export environments, such as PaPM Source_A, PaPM Source_B, and so on.

Let us imagine an example company that uses SAP PaPM Cloud SM and has three stages: Development, Test, and Production. This company has developed a new environment in the development stage, and now it needs to transport it into the test stage and, after successful testing, into the production stage. SAP cTMS can be used for precisely this purpose.

Let’s begin!

In your SAP PaPM Cloud SM application inside Content Management, you might be surprised to see the Transport Environment icon is disabled, even though you have an admin role with write access in your user.

The Transport Environment icon will be enabled once cTMS has been successfully subscribed to and configured. After setting up cTMS, you must create a landscape with nodes and routes, which will define the flow of how environments are transported.

For detailed instructions on how to set up the integration use SAP PAPM Cloud’s Admin Guide.

In this simple example, I created a landscape with transport nodes and routes. One virtual node named DEV_Node and two non-virtual nodes named TEST_Node and PROD_Node in cTMS.

The environment is first exported from the PaPM source tenant to the Virtual Node (DEV_Node). The virtual node functions as an object / transport group that bundles the environment before sending it to a specific target tenant. The idea is for the virtual node to transfer the environment to the first target tenant: TEST_Node and once this transfer is successful, the environment can then be moved to the next tenant: PROD_Node.

Now, I will walk you through the steps to perform environment transport using cTMS, based on the environment flow outlined in the above created landscape.

  1. In your SAP PaPM Cloud SM application inside Content Management, choose Transport Environment icon
  2. Select an environment that you want to transport
  3. Then choose CONFIRM
  4. In the Export to Cloud Transport Management dialog, choose the Transport Node. In this case, our virtual node DEV_Node
  5. Then choose EXPORT

NOTE: The transport node dropdown option will only display the nodes that are configured with the option Allow Upload to Nodes set to "Yes". For more information, see Create Transport Nodes.

Once successfully exported, the environment will now appear as a blob (Transport Request) in the DEV_Node Import queue. Since DEV_Node has a Forward Mode = Manual, there will be no automatic forwarding of transport requests to target tenants. However, if you wish to do it automatically, the options Pre-Import, Post-Import, or On Success can be maximized depending on your requirements.

Now in your SAP cTMS:

  1. Choose DEV_Node and go to the node’s import queue.
  2. Choose a transport request
  3. Then choose Import Selected
    💡    Alternatively, you can use the 'Import All' option as multiple transport requests can be selected and imported simultaneously
  4. Choose OK if you approve the transport from this source node into the next node or target tenant
  5. Choose the transport request and choose Forward
  6. Then choose OK

This will then forwarded the environment content from DEV_Node into TEST_Node.

To validate if the transport is successful, go to TEST_Node and go to the node’s import queue. The same transport request number should be moved and visible in the queue.

  1. Choose the transport request
  2. Choose Import Selected
  3. Then choose OK if you approve the transport to this target node

Depending on how you configured the URL in the destination, the content will either be imported or uploaded to the target PaPM Cloud Standard Model application.

Import

Use import in the URL if you want the environment to be automatically created as new in the target tenant during Cloud Transport Management import.

Example: https://<PaPMServiceKey.url>/sap/opu/odata/NXI/tms/import

⚠️ This will overwrite any existing environment with the same environment ID and version

Upload

Use upload in the URL if you want the environment to be only uploaded to the Content Management application of SAP Profitability and Performance Cloud Standard Model as a ZIP file, without immediate import. In this case, you need to manually import the environment later.

Example: https://<PaPMServiceKey.url>/sap/opu/odata/NXI/tms/upload

For this example, I used the “import” option in the URL, therefore the same Environment ID and Version has been created automatically in my Test tenant’s Environment (Main) Screen and there is no environment content uploaded in Content Management Screen. For “upload” option though, once you choose Forward, the environment content will first be uploaded to Content Management and you can then manually import the content.

After all the activities are finished in Test stage, you can perform the same steps to forward the environment content from Test into Production. That easy!

I hope this blogpost helped you with this additional option to have a smoother and secured transfer of environment contents from your source tenant to your target tenants with the help of cTMS integrated to your SAP PaPM Cloud – Standard Model.

entry.title: Re: SAP SuccessFactors Agent Lifecycle Management

The following statement is incorrect :"SAP SuccessFactors Agent Performance Management --> Formerly known as ProducerPro Enterprise(ICM)"

SAP SuccessFactors Agent Performance Management was previously named Agent Performance Management, enterprise edition.  It was also previously marketed as ICM.

ALM and APM do share a common code base that allows the products to share functionality.

entry.title: Re: Problem when calling a method from an include program
entry.content:
Raymond,

Thank you for your post.

I agree that, in most instances, raising an exception is the best approach.

However, in this particular method, returning either an initial value or a date related to the indicated table name is the most appropriate result. This method returns a “last processed” date. The idea is to use it in a code roughly similar to this (pseudo code):

 

last_date = zcl_pbi=>ultima_fecha_pbi( tabla = 'TABLE_TO_BE_PROCESSED' ).

SELECT * FROM TABLE_TO_BE_PROCESSED 
  WHERE table_date >= @last_date
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE(itab).
  " DO WHAT YOU NEED....
  " END OF TABLE PROCESS
  zcl_pbi=>update_last_date( date = last_date table = 'TABLE_TO_BE_PROCESSED' ).

 

Best Regards,

Luis

entry.content:
Dear Community,

we have set up principal propagation in general and it is working as expected.
Now we have a scenario, where the header field SSL_CLIENT_CERT is already set in the https request coming from the client. 
So that means, for https requests arriving on a certain port the header field SSL_CLIENT_CERT should not be filled by the web dispatcher with the client cert (which is standard procedure), but the existing content of header field SSL_CLIENT CERT should be forwarded to the backend. 
My questions are:

2. If the answer to 1. is yes: how would the modification rule look like?

Thank you
Stephan

entry.title: Re: CI-DS user creation and management
entry.content:
Here is the detailed information for Creation/Managing a User in CI DS

Security Administrators can add users to the system, activate them, and for standard users assign roles to them.

Log into the CI DS and then navigate to the Administration tab.

 

 

  1. In the Administration tab, click 

     

    (New User).

  1. Make one of the following selections:
    • To create a standard user who will have a role such as Developer or Production Operator, choose New User, then enter a first name, last name, and email address, and click Save. The language is automatically set to English.

  1. Activate the user by selecting the “Active” checkbox if you want the user to begin working in the system.
  2. Assign one or more roles to the user.
  3. Click Save.

You can notify new users that they have been made active in the system by clicking Send Activation Email within each new user's profile.

Control access to SAP Cloud Integration for data services functionality by assigning roles to your standard users.

Role

Authorizations

Production Operator

  • Executes and schedules tasks and processes in the Production environment.
  • Views tasks, processes, data flows, and datastore connections
  • Monitors running and finished tasks and processes from the Projects and Dashboard tabs

Administrator

  • Has all the abilities of a Production Operator.
  • Manages the registration of Data Services Agent instances.
  • Creates or modifies datastore connection information in the Production environment.
  • Promotes tasks and processes between environments, for example from Sandbox to Production
  • Can reset the cache of tasks and processes in Production

Developer

  • Creates and modifies tasks, processes, data flows, and datastore connections.
  • Executes and schedules tasks and processes in non-production environments such as Sandbox and views data to verify the results.
  • Can access only non-production environments, such as Sandbox

Security Administrator

  • Creates, activates, and deletes users.
  • Assigns roles to users.
  • Views security log

Site Administrator

  • Defines the organization's primary Security Administrator
  • Defines the connection to the SAP HANA instance

SAP Support

  • The SAP Support user role provides limited access to Sandbox and Production environments. Members of the SAP Support team are automatically assigned to this role to facilitate troubleshooting. For details, see SAP Support user role permissions.
  • The Security Administrator cannot assign or unassign users to this role but can add additional roles to the user.

After the Security Administrator creates a user within SAP Cloud Integration for data services, an account activation email is automatically sent from SAP Cloud Identity Service to the new user. If the user has an existing SAP Cloud Identity Service account, then those credentials are used.

After activating the SAP Cloud Identity Service account, the user can log into SAP Cloud Integration for data services in one of the following ways:

  • user credentials (email and password)
  • browser-based certificate

The certificate is issued by SAP for SAP web applications and must be imported to the browser.

For Detailed information about this you can check the SAP Help documentation
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration-for-data-services/security/create-user

entry.title: Migrating from ECC Org Structure (PPOMA/PPOME) to S/4HANA Teams & Responsibilities
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m currently working on a 1:1 migration project where we want to move our organizational structure from an ECC system (maintained via PPOMA/PPOME) over to the new Teams and Responsibilities approach in SAP S/4HANA (Fiori-based Flexible Workflow).

Goal: We need to replicate the existing org data—positions, relationships, cost center ownership, and approval responsibilities—so that we can leverage the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app in S/4HANA for Flexible Workflows.
Scale: It’s a large organizational structure, so manually recreating it is impractical.

1. Has anyone successfully performed an automated migration from PPOMA/PPOME (e.g., HRP1000/HRP1001 tables) into the new Teams & Responsibilities or Business Partner model used by S/4HANA Flexible Workflow?

2. Are there any SAP standard tools such as SAP Data Services, Legacy Transfer Migration Cockpit (LTMC), or Migration Cockpit that directly support migrating Org Management (OM) objects to Teams & Responsibilities?

3. If no standard solution is available, would a custom ABAP extractor or program (reading from PPOMA/PPOME tables) and Fiori OData APIs be the recommended approach to bulk-load the data into S/4HANA?

4. Any best practices or gotchas for preserving existing approval rules (e.g., PFAC responsibilities, KMDIMS, budget limits) so that they seamlessly transition to Flexible Workflow?

We’re aiming for as “1-to-1” a solution as possible. We recognize that there might need to be some re-mapping in S/4HANA, but if you have any experiences or guidance on how to minimize manual process, I’d greatly appreciate it.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: SAP Story Analytics Timesheet report
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm currently working on creating a timesheet table report in SAP Report Center, and I need some help resolving a data-related issue.

The report I'm building includes the following details:

  • Employee Name
  • Date
  • Category (e.g., Absence or Attendance)
  • Time Type (e.g., Working Time, Vacation, Sick Leave, etc.)
  • Number of Hours

The issue lies in how the data is presented:

  1. For attendance types, the data is recorded on a daily basis, which works perfectly for my needs.
  2. However, for absence types, the total number of hours is recorded as a single sum on the first day of the absence, instead of being distributed across the relevant days.

For example, as shown in the attached picture, if an employee requests a vacation for December 23rd and 24th, the report shows 14 hours on December 23rd but no data for December 24th
Has anyone encountered a similar issue, and if so, how did you handle it?

Any guidance or best practices would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: Invoices created via SD module does not show up in GS...
entry.content:
Hello,

Based on the information provided, the issue where export sale invoices created in the SD flow are not reflected in the GSTR1 report could be due to a configuration issue rather than a product bug. Here are a few steps you can take to troubleshoot and resolve the issue:

1. **Configuration Check**: Ensure that the necessary configurations for capturing export sales in the GSTR1 report are correctly set. This involves setting up your system to recognize export sale transactions separate from domestic ones and ensuring they're correctly captured in the GSTR1 report's relevant sections.

2. **GSTR1 Report Settings**: Verify if additional settings needed for export sales are configured so that such transactions are included when generating the GSTR1 report. This can be done through the Run Statutory Reports application.

3. **Reporting Preferences**: Make sure reporting preferences for the India GSTR1 report are defined correctly to include export sales invoices.

4. **Data Quality Check**: Double-check that all export sales documents (sales orders, deliveries, billings) are correctly completed and accounted for in the system before generating the GSTR1 report.

5. **Troubleshooting Tools**: Utilize the Extract Message Payloads report to analyze message payloads, which may help identify any underlying issues. Ensure you have the necessary authorizations to access these troubleshooting tools.

Warm regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Not Yet Published Object
entry.content:
Hello Chen,

Generally, these kinds of errors occur when you have performed some changes in the UI Designer or added some extension fields on the existing solution. This creates a change transaction of which are not yet published.

To resolve the issue, you have to save and activate the changed UI component in the UI Designer and then you can trigger an activation for whole solution again.

  1. Login to Cloud Applications Studio.
  2. Open the Solution.
  3. Open the Custom UI component on the UI Designer.
  4. Save and Activate it.

And in case, after performing these steps you are still getting a UI Designer exception, then please navigate to the Extensibility Explorer and publish these changes again, this should resolve this error.

Additionally, please refer to the below KBA for your reference.

2189207 - Error While Checking the PDI Solution: Not Yet Published Object

Best Regards,

Prathibha

entry.title: Re: 3rd Party process for configurable (KMAT) materials -...
entry.content:
Hello,

In the SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, specific item categories are used to handle different scenarios, such as third-party sales orders. For the public cloud version, the CBAB item category is used for trading goods (third-party bought in), typically for standard materials.

For configurable materials (KMAT), the standard third-party process generally isn't supported directly as it is for trading goods. Configurable products often require a different approach due to their complexity and potential variations in configuration.

Here are a few considerations and potential actions you could explore:

1. **Customizing Item Categories:**
In some scenarios, creating a custom item category might be necessary to tailor the process to fit your specific business needs. This might involve collaborating with an SAP consultant or partner who can tailor the third-party sales order process for configurable materials.

2. **Exploring Business Add-Ins (BAdIs):**
If your requirement extends beyond standard capabilities, implementing a BAdI could provide the flexibility needed for your specific business case. This would typically involve some degree of customization, which might be limited in the public cloud setting.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: SAP SQL Anywhere RESTORE with RENAME - What is the correct script for restoring database
entry.content:
Can you share the full engine console from the line "Starting Database"?

Archive backups have not changed in many years but is also not exercised much side as backup to tape declined in use.

I set up a testwhere I have a database with a system dbspace and 3 additional dbspaces like 

  • system\old\sysDb.db
  • dbs1\old\dbs1.dbs
  • dbs2\old\dbs2.dbs
  • dbs2\old\dbs2.dbs

An archive backup was created based on that directory structure.  To restore, the following was executed

restore database
    'C:\forum\system\new\sysDB.db'
from
    'C:\forum\archive\MyArchiveBackup'
rename dbs1 to 'c:\\forum\\dbs1\\new\\dbs1.dbs'
rename dbs2 to 'c:\\forum\\dbs2\\new\\dbs2.dbs'
rename dbs3 to 'c:\\forum\\dbs3\\new\\dbs3.dbs'

I then start the restored database as follows:

     dbeng17 -n NewDb c:\forum\system\new\sysDB.db

showing the following:

Starting database "sysDB" (C:\forum\system\new\sysDB.db) at Mon Jan 27 2025 09:18:22.353
Performance warning: Database file "C:\forum\system\new\sysDB.db" consists of 5 disk fragments
Opening dbspace 'dbs1' in file 'c:\\forum\\dbs1\\new\\dbs1.dbs' for database 'sysDB'
Opening dbspace 'dbs2' in file 'c:\\forum\\dbs2\\new\\dbs2.dbs' for database 'sysDB'
Opening dbspace 'dbs3' in file 'c:\\forum\\dbs3\\new\\dbs3.dbs' for database 'sysDB'

I would confirm that the database (reported in Starting database) is /new place/restored.db.

entry.title: Re: Visual Studio code fiori application deployment fails...
entry.content:
Hi all, thank you for the replies but I was able to fix it by following the below steps:

  1. Navigate to transaction /n/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE.
  2. Under ABAP_REPOSITORY_SRV change the processing mode from "co-deployment only" to "routing based" and add a system alias such as "LOCAL".
  3. Thereafter head back to VS Code and run ui5 build then npm run deploy and it should work

entry.title: Re: Assign Inspection Lot Origins to a custom Inspection...
entry.content:
The Configuration Activity 'Maintain Inspection Lot Origins and Assign Inspection Types' has following documentation in the configuration help :

"The inspection lot origins are predefined in the system. You cannot define your own inspection lot origins you can assign the inspection types you have created in the step before to an existing inspection lot origin."

 

In the Standard S/4HANA Cloud System the inspection lot origin 02 (Goods Issue) is not one of the predefined lot origins available and there is no scope item / expert configuration available to activate it

entry.title: Re: HTTP Call working in ECC but not working in S4
entry.content:
Hello @rohitsharma34673 

Not knowing much about the details of your scenario, I can only say the following:

  • I don't think the certificate is the issue here as you are using plain HTTP not secure HTTPS
  • It looks like S/4 server is not able resolve the domain name of the called host to an IP address. That is in the essence what "hostname unknown error" error is.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Control Editability of benefits based on the benefit...
entry.content:
We have improved the Benefits RBP so that customers can specify whether an employee or an administrator can enroll in benefits. However, it does not support at the benefit level, so if RBP is configured for admin to enroll or edit, it applies to all benefits; it cannot be configured for a subset of benefits.

As a workaround, the customer can use the following condition in the eligibility rule. This will prevent employees from enrolling in such benefits, however, once enrolled, the benefit will be visible to the employee, as will the Edit icon. But the system will not allow users to edit, only administrators can edit. Please check to see if this is feasible, and request that the customer test all of their use cases if they decide to follow this rule.

entry.title: Re: Post a 501 movement type using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content:
Hello @chris_mowl 

The 501 movement type is receipt from a vendor without a reference to a purchase order. Thus don't fill MOVE_* fields - they are for transfer postings. Fill MATERIAL, PLANT, STGE_LOC instead and VENDOR.

Also read the documentation to BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Automating Schema Name Management in CI/CD for CAP Ap...
entry.content:
Thank you for your answer. We tried with below approach and it worked. This might also be another solution.

.hdbsynonymconfig(db/cfg folder)

{
"com.sap::SYNONYM_NAME": {
"target": {
"object": "COM_CHS_ZCOM_DEMO",
"schema.configure": "ServiceName_1/schema"
}
}
}

Schema name will be taken from "schema" property of "sample-db-hdi" service instance.
This blog helped us here, https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/how-to-share-tables-across-different-cap-projects/ba-p/13497184 
Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Taking Groups to the Next Level with Application-Specific Groups
entry.content:
Thanks for the blog.

Have implemented it in my setup, but the Application Name is not updated. 

Situation. 

MS Entra was already present. Created the application MS entra. copied that id

Added the property ips.application.id to the source system. Btw ips.application.id was not in the dropdown list.

 

Added the section to the transformation 

"group": {
"ignore": false,
"mappings": [
{
"targetPath": "$.schemas[0]",
"constant": "urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:core:2.0:Group"
},
{
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group']['applicationId']",
"condition": "'%ips.application.id%' !== 'null'",
"constant": "%ips.application.id%"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group']['type']",
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group']['type']",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group']['supportedOperations']",
"targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:Group']['supportedOperations']",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.id",
"targetVariable": "entityIdSourceSystem"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.mailNickname",
"targetPath": "$.externalId",
"optional": true
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.displayName",
"targetPath": "$.displayName"
},
{
"sourcePath": "$.members",
"targetPath": "$.members",
"optional": true,
"preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
},
{
"targetPath": "$.members[*].id",
"constant": "value",
"optional": true,
"type": "rename"
}
]
}
}

 

Ran a job but nothing gets updated. Created a new group, ran job, still no application updated

 

Any ideas why?

entry.title: ABAP CDS Enum declaration and accessing values
entry.content:
I am trying to find how to access the Values from Enum in ABAP using the following example.

I have declared the following Enum

@EndUserText.label: 'Enum Value'
define type ze_testenum : abap.char(8) enum
{
  UNKNOWN = initial;
  Sun     = '7';
  Mon     = '1';
  Tue     = '2';
  Wed     = '3';
  Thu     = '4';
  Fri     = '5';
  Sat     = '6';
}

Next, I defined the following View Entity

define view entity ZI_TestEnum
  as select from I_CalendarDate
{
  key CalendarDate,
      ze_testenum.#Sun as Word
}
where
  CalendarDate = $session.system_date

The output of the CDS is

Next, I updated the View definition to:

define view entity ZI_TestEnum
  as select from I_CalendarDate
{
  key CalendarDate,
      ze_testenum.#Sun as Word,
      cast(ze_testenum.#Sun as abap.char(8)) as Word2
}
where
  CalendarDate = $session.system_date

The output of this CDS is:

Next, I tried to use the same method in ABAP using the following class

class zcl_je_test definition
  public
  final
  create public .

  public section.
    interfaces if_oo_adt_classrun.
  protected section.
  private section.
endclass.

class zcl_je_test implementation.
  method if_oo_adt_classrun~main.
    out->write( ze_testenum-mon ).
    out->write( conv string( ze_testenum-mon ) ).
  endmethod.
endclass.

Output of this class is:

Here in ABAP, casting doesn't convert the value of Mon to 1 unlike in CDS View definition. Why this difference in behavior?

I added the following statement in the Class

out->write( conv ze_testenum( '1' ) ).

Now the output of the class is

I tried the same in CDS view definition by adding the following statement

cast('2' as ze_testenum) as Word3

The output of CDS now is 

In summary, I am able to:

  1. Print/Output Enumerated Constant Name in both ABAP & CDS
  2. Convert Value to Constant Name in both ABAP & CDS
  3. Convert Constant Name to Value in CDS by Casting, whereas Casting in ABAP code doesn't convert the value

Question(1) : Is casting the correct method to access the defined fixed values (Enumerated Values) from the CDS?

Question (2): How do I convert Constant Name to Value in ABAP code?

Tagging blog owner here for better reach @AndreaUS

entry.title: Re: Using the Datasphere OData API with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Digging up this blog while trying to create import connections in SAC for a handful of Datasphere views... I have the same questions as sarthaksrivasta. Do we need separate connections for each invididual view that we need to import? Or is it possible to use a single OData connection to access more than one view in a single space in Datasphere?

If we were building our own client to use the OData API, we would be able to use the catalog endpoint to dynamically retrieve the assetRelationalDataUrl for each object we wish to import data from, but that logic doesn't appear to be possible within a connection in SAC.

entry.title: Re: How and where do you start a consulting career in SAP?
entry.content:
Hi, @David_Chaviano David and everyone!

I hope this message finds you well! My name is Koppany. I've decided to continue my career as an SAP consultant. I'm not making it the classical way because I have 15 years of work experience as a middle manager.

During this time, I have frequently encountered situations where even companies with over 100 years of experience can be shaken by a chain of poor decisions based on faulty information. These experiences motivate me. I have several years of experience with SAP. However, to make it easier to take the first step and join a trainee program, and to improve my chances in the selection process, I have obtained the C_IEE2E, C_TS410, and C_S4EWM certifications. I have a background in logistics, manufacturing, and network management. Unfortunately, I have not yet been successful in joining any trainee program. Do you have any advice on how I could earn additional points in the selection process?

Additional information: I live in Austria and speak English, German, and Hungarian.

Thank you in advance for your response! Have a nice day!
Koppány

entry.title: Re: Get the menu to the top of Business Application Studio
entry.content: This is one of the first things I always do when setting up my BAS dev space.  Unfortunately this setting is only available at the User level which means it is saved in the browser cache.  If you clear your browser cache you will need to reset this value in BAS the next time and it explains why you see it get overwritten from time to time.

entry.title: Re: Get the menu to the top of Business Application Studio
entry.content: My personal recommendation is to always try and make settings changes in the Remote tab in settings.  However, not all settings are available under Remote so some will need to be set at the User level.  This will store them within the dev space versus at the User level which is stored in the browser cache.

entry.title: Commitment management for CO Module
entry.content:
Hi, my client wants to the setup and optimization of Commitment Management in the SAP CO module. 

They would like to properly track the future expenditures using standard SAP transactions and reports (e.g., KSB2, KOB2) to see open Purchase Order obligations broken down by cost center or internal order. The main objectives are:

  • Viewing the original PO amount vs. invoice receipts to determine what is still open.
  • Using reports such as S_ALR_87013623 (for cost centers) and S_ALR_87013019 (for internal orders) to gain insight into both posted costs and commitments (BANF/PO) at any given point.

    I have already checked the above-mentioned reports, but the data for open commitments (i.e., original PO amount minus already received invoices) is not displayed as expected. I need the support to:

    1. Ensure Commitment Management is fully active in our Controlling area.
    2. Configure/verify any necessary master data or system settings.
    3. Enable the relevant standard reporting transactions so we can see accurate commitment values (not just actuals)

entry.title: News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 and more
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the availability of the updates to SAP Activate. This release significantly enhances the methodology guidance across a wide range of products and solution-specific content. These updates aim to streamline and improve the implementation process, providing a more seamless experience for our valued users. Here are the key highlights for this release. You can learn more in the detailed sections below:

  • SAP Activate Community Blogs and Activate Minute Videos
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates to SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Introducing SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade
  • Updates to SAP Activate for Conversion of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system to 3-system landscape
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2502 Release

 

SAP Activate Community | SAP Activate Blogs | SAP Activate Minute Videos

Join the SAP Activate Community to explore valuable content and resources while connecting with SAP partners, clients and internal colleagues by participating in discussions and sharing knowledge.

Make sure to follow the SAP Activate tag to access blog posts like those highlighted here, along with additional insights shared by SAP Activate experts within the community.

Recap of some blog posts from the community:

  • 12 Things that SAP implementation methodology SAP Activate tells you to do by Jan Musil
  • Discover SAP Activate Tips in a Minute with SAP Activate Minute Videos by Christina Galbreath
  • SAP Activate road map viewer Now Accessible via SAP for Me by Chandan Gurav
  • How to Begin an SAP Activate for Transformation to SAP Cloud ALM Project by Bob Byrne
  • Optimize SAP Implementation with SAP Activate Methodology and Guided Implementation in Cloud ALM by Ana Goetz
  • SAP Activate for RISE with SAP: Roadmap Updates & New Content!  by Adi Kamugisha
  • Providing Feedback in SAP Activate road map viewer via SAP for Me by Adi Kamugisha
  • Check out the new SAP Activate one minute video, SAP Activate Parallel Line Roadmap.

 

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition in 2502

The SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (3-system landscape) implementation roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are highlights for this release.

  • Updates to several tasks in the Project Management workstream and associated accelerators to refresh the procedures and content.
  • Reworked the Conduct Project Quality Gate tasks at the end of each phase. Updated the quality gate accelerator to prepare for the integration of the checklists into SAP Cloud ALM checklist functionality.
  • Added a new tag to easily identify deliverables and tasks associated with the GROW Adoption Plan.
  • Added two new tasks, Request Deployment Readiness Check for Upcoming Go-Live  and Review Business Processes with the CQC for Process Adoption Service, and updated task Request Knowledge Assessment to better support the services related to the continuous Quality Check program (CQC).
  • Added a new task Verify and Locate Provisioning Email from SAP. The task helps to ensure the Initial Admin locates and reviews the consolidated provisioning email from SAP once the request has been submitted via SAP For Me.
  • Migrated the Road Map viewer to the SAP for Me platform to provide a most consistent user experience with other sap support products. Renamed Roadmap Viewer to Road Map Viewer based on the recommendation from SAP One Voice.

GROW Adoption Plan

GROW Adoption Plan tag providing quick access to key adoption tasks

 

Updates of SAP Activate Methodology for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Activate Methodology content for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition continues to be enhanced to help guide the customer through the journey of a new implementation, system conversion, and selective data transition following the SAP Clean Core principles. These journeys are accessible by selecting the appropriate journey tag within the single roadmap in road map viewer or individually in SAP Cloud ALM.

Key highlights include:

  • In coordination with the Regional Implementation Group (RIG), several updates have been made in in the roadmaps pertaining to both System Conversion and Selective Data Transition.
  • For Extensibility in System Conversion Fit-to-Standard for Specific Features, User Experience Design and User Access and Security Planning and Design, new guidance and templates for managing functional specifications were added in the EXPLORE phase.
  • Updates have been made for all presentation guides for User Experience.
  • A new task, Review your Clean Core Assessment, for the Prepare, Explore, Realize, and Run phases under the deliverable Clean Core Quality Gate was added. This task provides guidelines for conducting a comprehensive review of the Clean Core Assessment to ensure alignment with Clean Core principles throughout the project activities.
  • Tasks pertaining to managing the SAP Business Network have been added including Self-Enable on SAP Business Network and Setup Integration for S/4 HANA System and SAP Business Network.

See the release notes for a complete list of all improvements.

 

Introducing SAP Activate for RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - Upgrade

This roadmap is designed to guide existing Private Cloud Edition customers (both direct and indirect) in transitioning to the latest release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. This enables customers to benefit from the latest innovations, including AI, enhance security and compliance, avoid costs associated with unnecessary custom code, and maximize the lifespan of their systems. As with the other RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition roadmaps, adherence to SAP Clean Core principles is embedded.

The content is already available in the SAP Activate road map viewer and SAP Cloud ALM.  Key pillars are:

Unleash Innovation

  • Increase productivity and improve business processes with advanced Business AI, automation and predictive analytics.
  • Enhance user experience and collaboration with Joule, SAP Fiori and Microsoft Teams integration.
  • Benefit from our continuous investment in business processes and integration technologies to facilitate the interaction in hybrid system environments.

Foster Security and Compliance

  • Stay secure with built-in cybersecurity and data protection based on the latest standards.
  • Improve risk and compliance transparency and always adhere to local and global regulations.
  • Respond to the latest compliance trends.
  • Prevent IT audits issue and be compliant with compatibility packs usage.

Build An Upgrade Routine

  • Optimize the upgrade strategy and benefit from greater flexibility and easier-to-consume innovation with the new release and maintenance strategy.
  • Lower upgrade impact by following SAP's clean core principles that make upgrades a seamless part of the software lifecycle by running on the latest release for regular adoption of innovation.
  • Upskill teams with the Enterprise Support Academy and dedicated SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Value Maps.

Leverage Tools and Services

  • Assess the upgrade impact by running SAP Readiness Check.
  • Reduce efforts and improve quality using Test Automation.
  • Benefit from SAP support guidance and resources included with RISE subscription for the technical upgrade execution.
  • Leverage services and support from SAP and its ecosystem.

Links:

  • RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition – Upgrade
  • Upgrade Community Page
  • White Paper

SAP Activate for upgrading RISE SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

 

Updates of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape 

The following are recent updates to the content based on insights from customer conversion projects, feedback from internal stakeholders, and customer-like testing activities.

  • Refreshed guidance for planning, receiving, and confirming the Test Data Refresh service. All tasks have been updated, new checklist has been added to reflect the new process for planning the service, preparing for its delivery, and confirming the service results. (link)
  • Updated accelerator Delta Features 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape that details the new capabilities of the 3-system landscape solution. You can access it on this task. (link)
  • Added new accelerator SAP BTP Guidance Framework to task Develop and Transport Side-by-Side Extensions in the Run phase. (link)
  • Updated procedure notes in tasks Confirm Conversion Project Schedule in the Discover phase to make sure Solution Builder customers consider the impact of specific activities on their conversion project. (link)
  • Added a new task Activate Scope Item 5XU to Use Document and Reporting Compliance in the Realize phase to guide customers using Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) features to create, process, and monitor electronic documents and statutory reports post-conversion. (link)
  • Updated task description and accelerators for Grant Access to Partner for Creating and Managing Cases in the Prepare phase. (link)

Refer to the methodology overview page in the SAP Activate road map viewer for the latest content updates and the overview video here. Additionally, bookmark the SAP Community page dedicated to conversion topics here.

 

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors

The SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are the highlights:

  • Updates to several tasks in the Project Management workstream and associated accelerators to refresh the procedures and content.
  • Task changes to improve results when combining with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition road map in SAP Cloud ALM.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2502 Release

In the 2502 release of the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, we are enhancing the Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) with a new app and various improvements. Key highlights include:

  • The new Post Upgrade Test (PUT) app offers various dashboards that visualize key factors of PUT execution. Additional enhancements include:
    • One click navigation to all applications used in PUT.
    • The ability to compare results from two Post Upgrade Test (PUT) runs.
    • Enhanced contextual information for Trigger and Tracking PUT runs.
    • Consolidated of all PUT related actions in once place (Consent | Maintain | Roles | Trigger).
  • Improved analytics in the intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) app provide overall and application area specific scope efficiency for the optimized scope.

For more details, refer to the blog on the 2502 features in the Test Automation Tool.

 

Run simple – SAP Activate – Accelerate and simplify the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

Your SAP Activate Team

entry.title: S/4 OnPrem - Is Key User Extensibility "Clean Core" but not compatible with ABAP Cloud?
entry.content:
I just started a totally new development for a new project on S/4 2023 FPS 01. I'm trying to follow the Clean Core approach to the extreme so I created a new Software Component and the structure of packages in it, everything using "ABAP Cloud" version.

Then I decided that for certain changes I would use the Extensibility Apps. For example, I wanted to create a list of values (aka Domain) using the "Custom Reusable Elements" app. I already encountered some limitation since it doesn't allow to enter a "blank" value with a description.

But most important, by default it got assigned to a non-transportable package, so I tried to use the app "Register Extensions for Transport" to reassign it to a package from my initial setup inside my SC.

My package was not available there. But that's not a problem, first it needs to be added via the app Configure Software Packages. My packages appear in the dropdown menu only after I changed the new software component in SE06 which adds it to the table DLV_SYSTC... (I found out this by debugging). But even if the package is available, then the following error comes "Package ZXXXXX can't be used. It is configured for ABAP language version 5." And I see the "reusable lists" generate DDIC CDS for example.

Therefore, my question is: should I assume that Key User Extensibility is Clean Core (future proof) but at the same time not compatible with ABAP Cloud? How should I organize the packages and software components then?

entry.title: Address from Object Activity does not have detailed address information
entry.content:
For our SAP B1 linked databases address linked to an activity does not have detailed address informations.

F.e. the postal code is empty, because in SAP B1 the activity does not have that field.
But the information is available in BP addresses, Equipment Card etc. (in SAP B1).

Is there a way that those values will be added automatically? (perhaps a missing UDF in SAP B1) or a simple way to solve this?

We are using the HTML offline report and having issues by printing the correct address, because the HTML template is building the address based on ServiceCall.activities.[0].address.

Best regards,

Alex
